response.justification: Bogdan is a strong generalist engineer with genuine staff-level breadth and recent, directly relevant MCP/agentic-workflow work at re:cinq. However, the role demands 8+ years of AI-driven solution delivery, and his GenAI exposure is concentrated in his current position (started April 2025, roughly 2 months at time of evaluation). Python — the dominant language in AI/ML engineering — is absent from his skills list, and there is no mention of RAG architectures, vector databases, or LLM evaluation pipelines anywhere in the resume. His background is more product/platform engineering than applied AI consulting, which is the core motion of a forward-deployed GenAI role.
strengths: Hands-on MCP (Model Context Protocol) server and agentic-workflow infrastructure work at re:cinq — one of the few directly on-point technical signals for this role
Proven staff-level scope across multiple organisations, including technical leadership, mentoring, and architecture ownership (Altom, re:cinq)
Cloud and data-pipeline experience via AWS CDK at Optilyz and high-throughput data-integration work at Dunnhumby, showing relevant infrastructure sensibility
concerns: Python is entirely absent from the stated skills and project history — a critical gap given it is the primary language for GenAI tooling, LLM frameworks, and data pipelines
No evidence of RAG architectures, vector databases, or LLM evaluation/observability work, which are explicit required qualifications
The 8-year AI-solution delivery requirement is not met; substantive GenAI work appears only in the current role, which is approximately two months old at evaluation time

key_factors.factor: GenAI tenure too short to meet 8-year requirement
